title: Could Working Fewer Hours Help Fight Obesity?
subtitle: New research suggests a link between long working hours and weight gain, sparking debates about the four-day work week.

summary.en: A recent study suggests that people who work longer hours are more likely to be obese. Researchers believe that stress and a lack of time for exercise are the main reasons for this trend. Experts are now calling for a four-day work week to help people live healthier lives. While some companies have already adopted this model, the government remains cautious about making it a requirement. The debate highlights the ongoing struggle to balance modern work demands with personal health.
summary.zh: 一項近期研究指出，工時較長的人更有可能出現肥胖問題。研究人員認為，壓力與缺乏運動時間是造成此趨勢的主要原因。專家們目前呼籲推動週休三日，以協助人們過上更健康的生活。雖然部分企業已經採用此模式，但政府對於將其列為強制要求仍持謹慎態度。這場辯論凸顯了在現代工作需求與個人健康之間取得平衡的持續掙扎。

paragraphs.en: A new study has brought attention to the relationship between how much we work and our physical health. According to The Guardian Health, research presented at the European Congress on Obesity suggests that working longer hours is linked to higher rates of obesity. The study looked at data from 33 countries between 1990 and 2022, comparing working patterns with weight-related health issues. The findings show that countries with longer annual working hours, such as the United States, Mexico, and Colombia, often have higher rates of obesity.
paragraphs.zh: 一項新研究引起了人們對工作時數與身體健康之間關係的關注。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，在歐洲肥胖症大會上發表的研究指出，工時較長與較高的肥胖率有關。該研究分析了 1990 年至 2022 年間 33 個國家的數據，比較了工作模式與體重相關的健康問題。研究結果顯示，年度工時較長的國家，例如美國、墨西哥和哥倫比亞，其肥胖率往往較高。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Pradeepa Korale-Gedara, the lead author of the study from the University of Queensland, explained that long hours often lead to increased stress. This stress raises the level of a hormone called cortisol in the body, which can cause people to store more fat. Furthermore, people working long hours often find themselves in jobs where they cannot move around or burn enough energy. Dr. Korale-Gedara noted that when people have a more balanced life, they experience less stress, which allows them to focus on eating better food and exercising more often.
paragraphs.zh: 昆士蘭大學（University of Queensland）的研究主筆普拉迪帕·科拉萊-格達拉（Pradeepa Korale-Gedara）博士解釋說，長時間工作通常會導致壓力增加。這種壓力會提高體內一種稱為皮質醇的荷爾蒙水平，進而導致人們儲存更多脂肪。此外，長時間工作的人往往從事無法走動或消耗足夠能量的工作。科拉萊-格達拉博士指出，當人們擁有更平衡的生活時，壓力會減少，這讓他們能專注於攝取更好的食物並增加運動頻率。

paragraphs.en: However, researchers are careful to point out that this study does not prove that working long hours is the only cause of obesity. Other factors, such as the average income level of a country, also play a significant role. Despite this, the study has encouraged experts to renew their arguments for a four-day work week. Supporters believe that giving people more time off could help them improve their health by moving away from unhealthy habits.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，研究人員謹慎地指出，這項研究並未證明長時間工作是導致肥胖的唯一原因。其他因素，例如國家的平均收入水平，也扮演了重要角色。儘管如此，這項研究仍促使專家們重新提出推動週休三日的論點。支持者認為，給予人們更多休息時間，有助於他們擺脫不健康的習慣，進而改善健康。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Rita Fontinha, a psychologist at the University of Reading, describes this issue as "time poverty." She explains that when people work very long hours or hold two jobs, they often do not have the energy to cook fresh meals. Instead, it becomes much easier to buy food that is packaged or processed, which is often less healthy. Dr. Fontinha believes that reducing working hours could lead to better choices regarding food, exercise, and sleep, ultimately contributing to a healthier society.
paragraphs.zh: 雷丁大學（University of Reading）的心理學家麗塔·方廷哈（Rita Fontinha）博士將此問題描述為「時間貧窮」。她解釋說，當人們工作時間過長或身兼兩職時，往往沒有精力烹飪新鮮餐點。相反地，購買包裝或加工食品變得容易許多，而這些食品通常較不健康。方廷哈博士認為，減少工時能讓人們在飲食、運動和睡眠方面做出更好的選擇，最終有助於建立一個更健康的社會。

paragraphs.en: In the United Kingdom, the idea of a four-day week is gaining some momentum. The Guardian Health reports that about 200 companies have already adopted this working pattern. Additionally, data from the Office for National Statistics shows that more than 200,000 workers have switched to a four-day week since the Covid-19 pandemic began. James Reeves, a campaign manager at the 4 Day Week Foundation, argues that a four-day week with full pay could significantly reduce obesity levels in Britain by giving people the time they need to make healthier choices. He suggests that the traditional five-day, nine-to-five work week is now 100 years old and is long overdue for an update.
paragraphs.zh: 在英國，週休三日的概念正逐漸獲得支持。據《衛報健康》報導，已有約 200 家企業採用了這種工作模式。此外，英國國家統計局（Office for National Statistics）的數據顯示，自新冠肺炎疫情爆發以來，已有超過 20 萬名員工轉為週休三日。週休三日基金會（4 Day Week Foundation）的專案經理詹姆斯·里夫斯（James Reeves）主張，在維持全薪的情況下實施週休三日，能透過給予人們做出更健康選擇所需的時間，顯著降低英國的肥胖率。他認為，傳統的週休二日、朝九晚五工作制已經實行 100 年了，早該進行更新。

paragraphs.en: Not everyone agrees that the government should force this change, however. While some local areas, such as the South Cambridgeshire district council, have introduced a four-day week for their staff, the national government remains opposed to making it a rule. A government spokesperson stated that they will not force companies to adopt a four-day week for five days of pay. Instead, the government prefers to use the Employment Rights Act to make it easier for employees to ask for flexible working arrangements. This allows workers to discuss their schedules with their employers without the government setting a strict national policy.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，並非所有人都同意政府應強制推行此項變革。雖然一些地方區域，例如南劍橋郡區議會（South Cambridgeshire district council），已為其員工引入了週休三日，但中央政府仍反對將其定為強制規定。政府發言人表示，他們不會強迫企業在維持五天薪資的情況下實施週休三日。相反地，政府傾向利用《就業權利法》（Employment Rights Act）來讓員工更容易申請彈性工作安排。這使得員工可以在不需政府制定嚴格國家政策的情況下，與雇主討論他們的工作時間表。

paragraphs.en: As the debate continues, the focus remains on how to balance the needs of businesses with the health of the workforce. While the link between working hours and obesity is becoming clearer, finding a solution that works for everyone remains a challenge. Whether a shorter work week becomes a standard practice or remains a choice for individual companies, the conversation shows that many people are looking for better ways to manage their time and their health in a busy world.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著辯論持續進行，焦點仍放在如何平衡企業需求與員工健康。雖然工時與肥胖之間的關聯性正變得越來越清晰，但找到一個對所有人皆適用的解決方案仍是一項挑戰。無論縮短工時最終成為標準做法，還是僅作為個別企業的選擇，這場對話顯示出許多人正在尋求在繁忙的世界中，以更好的方式管理自己的時間與健康。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make people notice or think about a specific subject.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語由 bring 衍生，意指「使某事受到重視」。文中：A new study has brought attention to the relationship between how much we work and our physical health.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have or keep a job or position.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「拿、握」，這裡指同時擁有兩份工作。文中：She explains that when people work very long hours or hold two jobs, they often do not have the energy to cook fresh meals.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The quality that keeps an idea or movement growing or developing faster.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原為物理學術語（動量），這裡引申為某個想法或趨勢正在變得越來越受歡迎。文中：In the United Kingdom, the idea of a four-day week is gaining some momentum.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Should have happened or been done a long time ago.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 早該發生的、延誤已久的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於圖書館借書逾期，這裡指某事早該進行卻拖延至今。文中：He suggests that the traditional five-day, nine-to-five work week is now 100 years old and is long overdue for an update.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Experts call for UK four-day week as study links long work hours to obesity

quiz.question: According to the study, what is one physiological reason why working long hours contributes to weight gain?

options.A: It forces employees to consume more processed food during office hours.
options.B: It increases cortisol levels, which encourages the body to store fat.
options.C: It reduces the average income level, making healthy food less affordable.
options.D: It prevents people from sleeping enough, which slows down their metabolism.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Dr. Pradeepa Korale-Gedara 明確指出長時間工作會導致壓力增加，進而提高體內皮質醇（cortisol）的濃度，這會促使身體儲存更多脂肪。選項 A 雖然在第四段提到（時間貧窮導致吃加工食品），但這不是第二段所解釋的生理機制。選項 C 在第三段提到是影響肥胖的因素之一，但與皮質醇無關。選項 D 雖然提到睡眠，但文章並未將其作為皮質醇導致肥胖的直接原因。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the British government's stance on the four-day work week?

options.A: They are planning to pass a law requiring all companies to adopt it by next year.
options.B: They believe that the current five-day work week is outdated and needs an immediate update.
options.C: They prefer to facilitate individual negotiations between employees and employers rather than mandating a policy.
options.D: They are actively discouraging companies from adopting flexible schedules to maintain national productivity.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，政府發言人明確表示不會強制要求企業實施四天工作制，而是傾向利用《就業權利法》（Employment Rights Act）讓員工更容易與雇主協商彈性工作安排。選項 A 與文意相反，政府明確表示不會強制。選項 B 是支持者（如 James Reeves）的觀點，而非政府的立場。選項 D 錯誤，因為政府其實是支持透過現行法律讓員工協商彈性工作的，並非阻礙。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'time poverty' refer to?

options.A: The inability to afford leisure activities due to low wages.
options.B: The lack of sufficient time to engage in healthy habits like cooking and exercising.
options.C: A state of financial hardship caused by working too few hours.
options.D: The feeling of being overwhelmed by modern technology at work.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段上下文，Dr. Rita Fontinha 解釋當人們工作時間過長或身兼數職時，他們沒有精力去烹飪新鮮餐點，這導致了「時間貧窮」。這意味著因為缺乏時間，人們被迫選擇較不健康的加工食品。選項 A 偏離了文章對於「時間」而非「金錢」的討論。選項 C 完全錯誤，因為文章討論的是工作過度而非工時過短。選項 D 並未在文中提及。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: The United Kingdom has successfully solved the problem of obesity through new labor laws.
options.B: Working long hours is the only proven cause of obesity in modern society.
options.C: There is a growing debate about how reducing work hours could improve public health and work-life balance.
options.D: Companies should ignore government regulations and set their own working schedules to save money.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心在於探討工作時數與肥胖之間的關聯性，並討論縮短工時（如四天工作週）作為改善健康與生活平衡的潛在解決方案，同時呈現了支持者與政府不同觀點的辯論。選項 A 誇大了事實，英國政府並未強制推行。選項 B 錯誤，第三段明確指出研究者承認還有其他因素（如收入水平）影響肥胖。選項 D 並非文章主旨，文章強調的是平衡企業需求與員工健康。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
